visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a close-up shot of a silver metal pole on an urban street corner. A blue and black pedestrian crossing button with an arrow pointing up is central. A hand enters the frame and presses the circular silver button. In the background, a grey asphalt road and a modern white building with large windows are visible under bright, natural daylight. (2) The hand remains in view, pointing towards the traffic lights across the street. The person starts counting with their fingers, raising them one by one until five fingers are displayed. The camera pans slightly to show the intersection with several cars and a yellow bus in motion. (3) The camera cuts to a medium shot of a young man wearing a grey beanie, a black long-sleeved shirt, and grey sweatpants. He stands next to the pole, looks at the camera with a playful expression, and presses the button multiple times rapidly. (4) As the signal changes, the man immediately begins to run across the white zebra crossing. The camera follows him from behind, showing his fast-paced movement across the wide street. Dry leaves are scattered near the curb in the foreground. (5) The man reaches the other side of the street, stops, and turns back toward the camera. He raises his arms in a 'shrug' or 'triumph' gesture with a wide smile and laughter. Large text overlays in pink and black appear on the screen, framing his reaction.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Slogan]: 澳洲雪梨 · 行人紅綠燈只有5秒?. (2) [Variety]: 雪梨都市傳說. [Variety]: 行人紅綠燈只有5秒.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main creator performing for the camera.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young East Asian male with a grey knit beanie, a black long-sleeved t-shirt, and light grey baggy sweatpants with a black side stripe.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A modern metropolitan street intersection in Sydney with high-rise commercial buildings, clear road markings, traffic lights, and urban infrastructure like poles and trash bins.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio begins with an energetic male voice in Mandarin introducing a 'Sydney urban legend' over an upbeat, four-on-the-floor EDM background track with bright synth stabs. (2) A distinct mechanical 'click' is heard as the pedestrian button is pressed. The speaker's tone becomes highly animated. (3) The speaker begins counting 'one, two, three, four, five' in Mandarin, his voice rising in volume and pitch with each number, synchronized with the rhythmic pulse of the BGM. (4) Multiple rapid 'clicking' sounds follow, indicating repeated button presses. The man shouts 'Kaishi!' (Start!) with high arousal. (5) Rhythmic, percussive sounds of footsteps running on a hard surface dominate the mix for a few seconds. (6) The video concludes with the speaker's high-pitched, joyful laughter and a final crescendo in the electronic music.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To humorously share a personal observation about the rapid timing of pedestrian traffic lights in Sydney, inviting viewers to relate to the 'urban legend' of having to run to cross the street in time.

high_inference.freeDescribe: A typical social-media style 'fun fact' or 'life in...' vlog that uses high-energy delivery and physical comedy to engage the audience.
